title: hordeum sativum subsp. distichon
intro: Hordeum sativum subsp. distichon, also known as two-rowed barley, is a subspecies of the Hordeum sativum plant, which is a type of cereal grain belonging to the grass family. Traditionally, the grains of two-rowed barley have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hordeum sativum subsp. distichon include supporting digestive health, reducing inflammation, and acting as a natural diuretic. Hordeum sativum subsp. distichon can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used as a natural ingredient in various food products, such as soups, stews, and malted beverages.
